what_it_does: Defines a single ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict declaring a 'sessions' attachment type: a session addressed as session://<id> attached to a channel as context / a recall source (the §3 manifest sessions bucket), target_kind='address', multi=True.
observations: desc states the session serves as context and as a recall source for the channel.
multi=True allows many session attachments per channel.
standouts: Frames an attached session as both general context and a recall source, linking to the recall scope concept.
conventions: Same single-dict descriptor pattern as siblings.
Inline comment labels the session:// address scheme for target_kind.
notes: Registry entry for 'sessions': session://<id> addresses attached as channel context and recall sources, many-per-channel.
questions: How does a session attached as a recall source relate to the single recall-scope selector in recall.py?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the sessions attachment type as the docstring states.
intention_signals: Aims to let channels attach prior sessions as addressed context and recall sources so a channel carries relevant session history.
novelty: The dual role of an attached session as both context and recall source under one address scheme.
contribution: Contributes the declarative definition of the sessions attachment type, its session:// address scheme, and its context/recall-source role.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptor module declaring the 'sessions' channel attachment type: session://<id> addresses attached as context and recall sources, multi=True.
intention_for_embedding: To register prior sessions as channel-bound addressed attachments serving as context and recall sources.
